sketches.mechanism: The `object` declaration creates a singleton — exactly one instance can ever exist, and it is both the type and the value.
sketches.resonance: Loneliness is not a state but a structural guarantee enforced by the language: there can only ever be one.

sketches.mechanism: A generator function that yields once and then returns — it is exhausted after a single iteration, and calling next() again raises StopIteration.
sketches.resonance: A first meeting that the runtime permits exactly once; every attempt to revisit it is an exception.
sketches.draft:
def first_meeting():
    yield True

us = first_meeting()
next(us)
next(us)

sketches.mechanism: In Elixir, variables are immutable bindings; rebinding creates a new binding but the old value persists unchanged in any process that already captured it.
sketches.resonance: You can move on and rebind yourself to something new, but whoever already held the old you will never see you change.
sketches.draft:
self = :who_i_was

spawn(fn ->
  Process.sleep(:infinity)
  self
end)

self = :who_i_became
